description_long: This house features a tall, narrow main structure with a steeply pitched gable roof and a secondary, lower extension attached to one side. The exterior is clad in a combination of vertical wooden planks and stonework at the ground level, giving it a rustic, traditional appearance. Multiple rectangular windows with white frames are symmetrically arranged on the upper stories, while the lower level includes a stone archway entry and a set of stone steps leading up to the main door. The roof is dark and textured, with the main section curving slightly at the peak, and the side extension has a simpler, sloped roof. A small balcony with a metal railing is visible on one side, and the house sits elevated above ground level, accessible by a prominent staircase. The overall design suggests a blend of medieval and cottage architectural influences, suitable for a quaint urban or village setting.
description: A tall, narrow house with a steep gable roof, stone base, and wooden upper stories, featuring multiple windows, a balcony, and a prominent staircase. The structure combines rustic stonework with traditional wood cladding for a charming, old-world appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the tall main facade with two rows of windows, stone base, and a staircase ascending along the left side.
description_view_1: Direct view of the main entrance with a stone archway, symmetrical window arrangement, and the steep gable roof above.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the balcony, a single window on the upper story, and the attached side extension with a sloped roof.
description_view_3: Side view displaying the secondary structure with its own windows and roof, as well as the staircase leading up to the main entrance.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with some asymmetrical placement on the extension
attribute_entryway_design: Stone archway entry with steps, no porch, and a simple wooden door
